---
title: "Boot menu detect my External Hard Disk"
date: 2011-01-14
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by Anime4000 on 2011-01-14
I have update ubuntu on laptop, but during updating, i see the progress, but I see ubuntu detect my extHDD as Windows NT. Yes, my extHDD are bootable because BartPE on it, so how do I remove from list?

i have open
```

gksu gedit /boot/grub/grub.cfg

```
seem C programming look like, before I do anything false, which one I should remove?

here the list:
[code]
#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE
#
# It is automatically generated by grub-mkconfig using templates
# from /etc/grub.d and settings from /etc/default/grub
#

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/00_header ###
if [ -s $prefix/grubenv ]; then
  set have_grubenv=true
  load_env
fi
set default="0"
if [ "${prev_saved_entry}" ]; then
  set saved_entry="${prev_saved_entry}"
  save_env saved_entry
  set prev_saved_entry=
  save_env prev_saved_entry
  set boot_once=true
fi

function savedefault {
  if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then
    saved_entry="${chosen}"
    save_env saved_entry
  fi
}

function recordfail {
  set recordfail=1
  if [ -n "${have_grubenv}" ]; then if [ -z "${boot_once}" ]; then save_env recordfail; fi; fi
}

function load_video {
  insmod vbe
  insmod vga
}

insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
if loadfont /usr/share/grub/unicode.pf2 ; then
  set gfxmode=640x480
  load_video
  insmod gfxterm
fi
terminal_output gfxterm
insmod part_msdos
insmod ext2
set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
set locale_dir=($root)/boot/grub/locale
set lang=en
insmod gettext
if [ "${recordfail}" = 1 ]; then
  set timeout=-1
else
  set timeout=0
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/00_header ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###
set menu_color_normal=white/black
set menu_color_highlight=black/light-gray
### END /etc/grub.d/05_debian_theme ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-24-generic' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-24-generic root=UUID=90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219 ro   quiet splash
	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-24-generic
}
menuentry 'Ubuntu, with Linux 2.6.35-24-generic (recovery mode)' --class ubuntu --class gnu-linux --class gnu --class os {
	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
	echo	'Loading Linux 2.6.35-24-generic ...'
	linux	/boot/vmlinuz-2.6.35-24-generic root=UUID=90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219 ro single

	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-24-generic
}

	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}

	recordfail
	insmod part_msdos

	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'

	initrd	/boot/initrd.img-2.6.35-22-generic
}
### END /etc/grub.d/10_linux ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###
### END /etc/grub.d/20_linux_xen ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin
}
menuentry "Memory test (memtest86+, serial console 115200)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod ext2
	set root='(hd0,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set 90a772ef-1014-4c00-be8b-34458f054219
	linux16	/boot/memtest86+.bin console=ttyS0,115200n8
}
### END /etc/grub.d/20_memtest86+ ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###
menuentry "Windows NT/2000/XP (on /dev/sdc1)" {
	insmod part_msdos
	insmod fat
	set root='(hd2,msdos1)'
	search --no-floppy --fs-uuid --set b4fe-5315
	drivemap -s (hd0) ${root}
	chainloader +1
}
### END /etc/grub.d/30_os-prober ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###
# This file provides an easy way to add custom menu entries.  Simply type the
# menu entries you want to add after this comment.  Be careful not to change
# the 'exec tail' line above.
### END /etc/grub.d/40_custom ###

### BEGIN /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
if [ -f  $prefix/custom.cfg ]; then
  source $prefix/custom.cfg;
fi
### END /etc/grub.d/41_custom ###
[code]

---

### Post by quixote on 2011-01-15
Don't edit that file. (That's what "# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE" means :biggrin:)

The directory /etc/grub.d/ has files in it that you can change to add grub menu entries. For instance, the /etc/grub.d/40_custom file mentioned at the end of the list has the entries for other OSes.  If that's the partition you *don't* want, then you can delete it in 40_custom.

Other than that, though, I'm not sure how you'd remove a grub entry. There are also boot settings in the file /etc/default/grub.  One of those two is where you need to make the changes, but unfortunately it's been a while since I had to fight with grub, and I don't remember what to suggest.  Still, maybe it'll help your search.

Once you have the changes you want, you run "sudo update-grub" (without quotes), and that writes the changes to the grub.cfg file.

---
